![image](data/imgs/header_logo.png)

### 🌸 Chemist's interface to molecular data generated by GenProSyn GFlowNet model 🌸

---

_Author: Hubert Rybka_

## 1. Requirements

- Python 3.13
- [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) for dependency management

## 2. Setup

```bash
uv sync
```
This creates a `.venv` and installs all dependencies pinned in `uv.lock`.

## 3. Hosting on LAN

To make the app reachable from other machines on the local network, bind it to all network interfaces instead of just localhost. The included `launch.sh` does this:

```bash
uv run streamlit run app.py --server.address=0.0.0.0 --server.port=8501
```

Once running, other devices on the same network can connect at:

```
http://<server-lan-ip>:8501
```

Find the server's LAN IP with `hostname -i` (Linux) or `ipconfig` (Windows), and make sure port `8501` is allowed through the server's firewall.

> Note: session state (loaded dataset, favourites, browsing position) is per-browser-session and is **cleared when the browser tab/session ends**. Datasets saved to the library persist on the server and are shared by everyone.

## 4. Data format

Uploaded `.csv` files must contain at least two columns:

- `molecule` — the SMILES string of the molecule
- `path` — the synthesis pathway, as a stringified list alternating `[substrate, (reaction_smarts, reagent_or_None), product, ...]`

The app reads and writes to the following directories:

- `data/library/` — datasets saved from the app for quick reloading (`.pkl` files)
- `data/user_reports/` — bug reports
